author: mratsim
content:
> <@!570268431522201601>  I was not sure for BN, but that’s great. In practice these checks based on endomorphism were not implemented in my Rust code base and were not suggested for 1962 cause it potentially may be touching a patent for fast multiplication based on endomorphism
<@!381789485777682434> patents expired yesterday https://patents.google.com/patent/US7110538B2/en, https://patents.google.com/patent/US20060029222

author: shamatar
content: Yep 🙂 but current implementation that are part of Geth/OE/Nethermind didn’t have fast checks implemented, so (depending on a time frame) if we decide that it’s good to have for Berlin then it needs to be quite rushed. Benefits: lower gas costs and it doesn’t break any existing test (neither positive nor negative), downside: code has to be written:)

author: shamatar
content: That would be great to coordinate this work with Celo so we can indeed get a consistent and free re-testing and re-fuzzing on both BLS12-381 and BLS12-377

author: mratsim
content: btw, what kind of speed do you get on scalar mul and pairing? I just finished implementing pairings from scratch for BN254-Snarks, BN254-Nogami and BLS12-381 the past 3 days, currently doing BLS12-377 but that sqrt is killing me.

author: shamatar
content: I didn’t implement square roots, but can later today or tomorrow send you pairing and multiplication (double and add by 2^256 - 1) benchmarks from 1962 for at least BLSes

author: mratsim
content: so how do you hash to curve?

author: mratsim
content:
My latest benches for BLS12-381 are here, though I still have several optims to make: https://github.com/mratsim/constantine/pull/90#issue-493406885

scalar mul G1 79µs, scalar mul G2 219µs, pairing 884 µs

author: shamatar
content: I've sent the numbers to DM to not spam here
